name: deserialize_always_none
reason: If entailed, deserialize can never successfully produce a CKeyHashMap, making the spec vacuously true on success

name: deserialize_result_empty_map
code:
proof fn phi_2_deserialize_result_empty_map(data: Vec<u8>, start: usize, x: CKeyHashMap, end: usize)
    requires
        x.is_marshalable(),
        start <= end <= data.len(),
        data@.subrange(start as int, end as int) == x.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        x.spec_to_vec()@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, every successfully deserialized CKeyHashMap is empty, meaning non-trivial maps can never round-trip

name: deserialize_zero_bytes_consumed
code:
proof fn phi_3_deserialize_zero_bytes_consumed(data: Vec<u8>, start: usize, x: CKeyHashMap, end: usize)
    requires
        x.is_marshalable(),
        start <= end <= data.len(),
        data@.subrange(start as int, end as int) == x.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        start == end,
{
}
reason: If entailed, the serialized form is always zero-length, contradicting the length-prefix encoding of Vec<CKeyKV>

name: to_vec_always_empty
reason: If entailed, spec_to_vec is trivially constant, meaning CKeyHashMap can never faithfully represent any key-value data

name: never_marshalable
reason: If entailed, no CKeyHashMap instance can ever satisfy is_marshalable, so serialize/deserialize round-tripping is impossible for all inputs
